skill.name: ai-visual-accuracy-check
skill.description: Compares rendered HTML screenshots against source PDF page images using AI visual reasoning and a configurable quality gate.
skill.summary: Validate PDF-to-HTML visual fidelity with AI scoring and pass/fail guidance.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer's Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, filenames, and ordinary prose. The skill still has medium operational risk because its intended workflow reads local HTML and image files, renders screenshots with a headless browser, and sends visual content to Claude for analysis.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party AI Visual Analysis Sends Document Images
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs the assistant to attach the original PDF PNG and rendered HTML screenshot to Claude for comparison. This is legitimate for the skill, but it may expose sensitive document pages to an external AI service if users provide confidential PDFs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented process explicitly sends both images to Claude for multimodal comparison. The behavior is intentional, but it creates a clear data-sharing risk.

medium_findings.title: Local File Rendering and Report Output
medium_findings.description: The skill reads local HTML and PDF page image files, renders HTML through a headless browser, and saves a report under an output directory. This is expected behavior, but users should scope input and output paths to the project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads, browser rendering step, and report path are directly documented. No malicious path traversal or secret collection is shown.

low_findings.title: Static Ruby Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown inline code, fenced code blocks, examples, diagrams, and file paths. I did not find Ruby code, shell backtick execution, or user-controlled command construction in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax occurs in Markdown documentation and JSON or bash examples, not executable Ruby. There is no script file or dynamic command invocation in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections correspond to the word AI and descriptive visual reasoning text, not cryptographic algorithms or hashing code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions of AI visual reasoning. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, algorithms, or security-sensitive hashing behavior.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged ordinary validation and comparison prose as reconnaissance. No evidence found of OS discovery, environment probing, host enumeration, or network scanning instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss retrying invalid AI output and the flexibility of visual comparison. They do not instruct system inspection or reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Headless Browser Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends rendering HTML with Playwright or Selenium. Browser automation is expected for screenshot capture, but it should run against trusted local files or sandboxed content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The browser automation requirement is explicit. Risk depends on the trust level of the HTML being rendered.

content.value_statement: PDF-to-HTML conversions can look correct in markup while losing layout, hierarchy, or spacing. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through screenshot-based visual review with scored pass/fail output.
actual_capabilities: Defines inputs for generated HTML, original PDF page images, output directory, chapter metadata, and score threshold.
Guides rendering of HTML into a screenshot with a headless browser such as Playwright or Selenium.
Provides a detailed visual comparison prompt for Claude with weighted layout, hierarchy, positioning, and typography criteria.
Specifies a structured report with scores, differences, confidence, explanation, and recommendation.
Defines a blocking quality gate where scores below 85 stop the pipeline.
Supports multi-page chapter review through key-page comparison and averaged scoring.
limitations: The skill is procedural documentation and does not include an executable implementation.
Visual scoring depends on the AI model and may vary between runs.
Sensitive PDF pages or screenshots may be sent to an external AI service during review.
It focuses on visual fidelity and does not verify semantic HTML quality or accessibility compliance.

use_cases.title: Check converted textbook chapters
use_cases.description: Compare generated chapter HTML against source PDF page images before release.

use_cases.title: Gate document conversion pipelines
use_cases.description: Add a score threshold that blocks deployment when visual fidelity falls below the expected level.

use_cases.title: Review layout regressions
use_cases.description: Use scored visual feedback to identify spacing, hierarchy, or positioning changes after CSS updates.

prompt_templates.title: Run a single-page check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of one rendered page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-visual-accuracy-check to compare chapter_02.html with 02_page_16.png. Use the default 85 threshold and summarize the pass or fail result.

prompt_templates.title: Review a chapter sample
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-visual-accuracy-check on the opening, middle, and final pages for chapter 4. Average the page scores and list the main visual differences.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a failed gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting after a low score.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-visual-accuracy-check to explain why the latest chapter failed the 85 visual threshold. Prioritize layout, hierarchy, positioning, and typography fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare conversion variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of multiple generated HTML versions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-visual-accuracy-check to compare two rendered HTML versions against the same PDF page. Recommend the version with stronger visual fidelity and explain the tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Compare a rendered chapter page to its source PDF page image with the default threshold.
output_examples.output: The page passes with a high visual similarity score. Layout and hierarchy match well, with only minor spacing differences.

output_examples.input: Review a chapter after CSS changes caused layout shifts.
output_examples.output: The page fails the threshold. The main issues are heading prominence, list indentation, and table alignment compared with the PDF.

output_examples.input: Evaluate three representative pages from a multi-page chapter.
output_examples.output: The average score is acceptable. The opening page is strongest, while the final page needs minor typography adjustments.

best_practices: Use non-confidential samples unless your AI service policy allows document image sharing.
Keep the default 85 threshold until you have project-specific calibration data.
Store original PDF images and rendered screenshots with stable names for repeatable review.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the AI score as a replacement for accessibility or content correctness checks.
Do not lower the threshold to pass known layout defects without documenting the reason.
Do not render untrusted HTML outside a sandboxed browser environment.

faq.question: What does this skill compare?
faq.answer: It compares a rendered HTML screenshot with an original PDF page image.

faq.question: Does it require Claude vision support?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow expects a multimodal model that can inspect both images.

faq.question: What score passes the gate?
faq.answer: The default pass threshold is 85 out of 100.

faq.question: Can it handle multi-page chapters?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends comparing key pages and averaging their scores.

faq.question: Does it perform pixel-perfect comparison?
faq.answer: No. It uses contextual visual judgment rather than strict pixel differences.

faq.question: What data should users protect?
faq.answer: Users should protect confidential PDF pages, rendered screenshots, and generated reports.
